problem: `gates.cvar_gate` is passing while `metrics.cvar95` is materially worse than `gates.cvar_threshold_R` in recent options-MC artifacts (e.g., cvar95=-1.7103 vs threshold=-1.0 with cvar_gate=true). This is a high-impact risk-control contract break: tail-risk gating can be misinterpreted as healthy by downstream rankers/alerts, degrading reliability and potential profitability decisions.
required_change: Define and enforce one explicit CVaR gate contract: gate passes iff the chosen CVaR metric is at/above threshold under a single documented orientation (no mixed sign semantics).
Align `gates.cvar_gate`, markdown text, and JSON field naming/units so they cannot disagree (e.g., `cvar95_R` vs threshold in same unit).
Add invariant checks in artifact generation that fail closed when gate truth value is inconsistent with metric-threshold comparison.
Expose a machine-readable `cvar_gate_reason` (pass/fail with compared values) for downstream diagnostics.
acceptance_tests: Unit test: boundary cases for CVaR gate (cvar below, equal, above threshold) produce correct boolean outcomes under documented orientation.
Unit test: generated artifact cannot have `cvar_gate=true` when compared metric violates threshold; invariant raises explicit error/fail-closed state.
Regression test over latest 200 options-MC artifacts (or synthetic replay) asserts zero contract violations of the rule used by production gate evaluator.
Integration test: markdown gate line mirrors JSON gate decision and includes compared values (metric + threshold) for auditability.
Integration test: downstream ranking/selection path ignores or blocks artifacts when CVaR gate invariant fails (no silent promotion).
constraints: Do not relax existing risk thresholds.
Keep backward compatibility for existing consumers where possible; if fields are renamed, provide compatibility alias/versioned schema note.
Fail closed on ambiguity or unit mismatch.
